what_it_does: This JSON file is an interpretive ledger entry that summarises and indexes a markdown contract document describing the System Map visual editor and its disk-write adapter: it records what the contract covers, notable observations, safety requirements, open questions, and embedding summaries.
observations: It records metadata for an analysed source document: project 'claude-ds', source path 'analysis/SYSTEM-MAP-EDITOR-ADAPTER.md', and model 'opus'.
It summarises an operation queue JSON schema with a generatedFrom staleness timestamp and an ordered ops array of createFolder, createFile, rename, duplicate, move, archive, and delete.
It states that all paths are project-relative and match node id/path values in system-map.json.
It lists an op-to-filesystem-action mapping (mkdir -p, empty-file write, mv, cp -r, rm -rf, archive into _archive/).
It notes correctness requirements: staleness guard comparing generatedFrom to current generatedAt, idempotent creates, refusal to overwrite on mv/cp, and destructive delete handling.
It warns never to move or delete compiler artifacts (_ds_bundle.js, _ds_manifest.json, _adherence.oxlintrc.json) and to re-run check_design_system after moving components.
It describes in-memory single-sourcing: edits persist to localStorage['cv-sysmap-edits-v1'], Rescan re-reads disk, Clear discards, and a 6-second auto-poll is suppressed while edits are pending.
It includes a recipe for building a minimal Node adapter and stresses that the op vocabulary must stay in sync between the contract and the editor's emit calls.
It ends with an empty symbols array and explicit questions about whether the referenced build-system-map.js, editor emit code, and Node adapter implementation exist.
standouts: Frames the browser-preview-cannot-write constraint as a typed operation queue plus external-adapter replay pattern.
Highlights a staleness/conflict guard using generatedFrom versus generatedAt timestamps to avoid silent clobbering.
Flags the delete operation mapping to rm -rf as a destructive default with only a soft trash-dir suggestion.
conventions: Follows the interpretive ledger schema with project, path, model, symbols, and embedding fields.
Maintains neutral, descriptive language without ranking the analysed contract against others.
Uses the 'Piece N' framing implied by the source path being part of a larger System Map series.
Reflects the project's one-home / single-source discipline by noting the op vocabulary must be changed in the contract and editor together.
concerns: delete maps to rm -rf with only a soft suggestion to use a trash dir — destructive default with no enforced undo.
The file itself is an interpretation, not the source contract, so its claims about implementation status are unverified from this file alone.
The empty symbols array means no code symbols were extracted, leaving the analysis at the document level only.
notes: A structured interpretive summary of the System Map editor/adapter contract, capturing the op queue schema, safety guards, and open implementation questions.
questions: Does the source markdown file 'analysis/SYSTEM-MAP-EDITOR-ADAPTER.md' exist at the recorded path, or is this ledger entry describing a planned contract?
Where are build-system-map.js and the editor emit code relative to this ledger path, and do they implement every operation listed?
Is there a reference Node adapter implementation, or is the contract still awaiting one?
What does the 'claude-ds' project prefix signify within the larger company ledger?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file declares itself an interpretive analysis of the editor/adapter contract and contains the expected summary fields, observations, safety notes, and embedding metadata.
apparent_themes: Operation queue as single record of intent
Staleness and conflict safety on shared disk state
intention_signals: To preserve the design intent and safety contract of a browser-only visual editor that reorganises a real codebase by emitting a replayable operation queue for an out-of-band filesystem adapter.
novelty: It captures the unusual visual-codebase-reorganiser-without-write-access pattern: an in-memory, FLIP-animated tree editor that emits a typed, ordered op queue for an external adapter to enact.
contribution: Contributes a structured interpretive record of the editor/adapter contract, including the operation vocabulary, staleness/overwrite guards, compiler-artifact prohibitions, re-sourcing procedure, and open implementation questions, which a fused system could keep as metadata for that seam.
summary_for_embedding: Interpretive ledger JSON summarising a System Map visual editor contract that specifies a typed, ordered, staleness-guarded file-operation queue replayed by an external disk-write adapter to reorganise a codebase non-destructively.
intention_for_embedding: To capture the design intent and safety contract of a browser-only editor-to-filesystem adapter seam for later fusion.
